message:
fix(ci): Check CPython test output instead of exit code

`python3 -m test` can return non-zero exit code even when tests pass
(e.g., when tests are skipped or have resource warnings). This caused
false failures like "Tests failed: 1/95" when all tests actually passed.

Fix: Check for "Tests result: SUCCESS" in output instead of exit code.

Applies to: core1, core2, core3, linux, macos CPython test jobs.

files.patch:
@@ -481,7 +481,10 @@ jobs:
             [ -z "$test" ] && continue
             TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
             # Use Python's built-in test module
-            if python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
+            # Check output for SUCCESS instead of exit code (some tests return non-zero even when passing)
+            OUTPUT=$(python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1) || true
+            echo "$OUTPUT"
+            if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Tests result: SUCCESS"; then
               PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
             else
               FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
@@ -553,7 +556,10 @@ jobs:
             [ -z "$test" ] && continue
             TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
             # Use Python's built-in test module
-            if python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
+            # Check output for SUCCESS instead of exit code (some tests return non-zero even when passing)
+            OUTPUT=$(python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1) || true
+            echo "$OUTPUT"
+            if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Tests result: SUCCESS"; then
               PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
             else
               FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
@@ -625,7 +631,10 @@ jobs:
             [ -z "$test" ] && continue
             TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
             # Use Python's built-in test module
-            if python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
+            # Check output for SUCCESS instead of exit code (some tests return non-zero even when passing)
+            OUTPUT=$(python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1) || true
+            echo "$OUTPUT"
+            if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Tests result: SUCCESS"; then
               PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
             else
               FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))

             [ -z "$test" ] && continue
             TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
             # Use Python's built-in test module
-            if python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
+            # Check output for SUCCESS instead of exit code (some tests return non-zero even when passing)
+            OUTPUT=$(python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1) || true
+            echo "$OUTPUT"
+            if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Tests result: SUCCESS"; then
               PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
             else
               FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
@@ -766,7 +778,10 @@ jobs:
             [ -z "$test" ] && continue
             TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
             # Use Python's built-in test module
-            if python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1; then
+            # Check output for SUCCESS instead of exit code (some tests return non-zero even when passing)
+            OUTPUT=$(python3 -m test test_${test} 2>&1) || true
+            echo "$OUTPUT"
+            if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Tests result: SUCCESS"; then
               PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
             else
               FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
